expectations.text: behavioral_check: Identifies speed_threshold as a movement-exclusion cutoff (events kept when speed <= threshold)
expectations.evidence: States 'A candidate is kept only when the immobility condition holds at both endpoints, i.e. speed <= speed_threshold at the start AND at the end of the event.'

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Predicts the direction correctly: raising 4 -> 10 keeps MORE candidate events, not fewer
expectations.evidence: Explicit: 'Bumping speed_threshold from 4.0 to 10.0 cm/s loosens the immobility filter, so you keep more candidate events.'

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Identifies the tradeoff: looser immobility conditioning admits peri-movement events that may not be true SWRs
expectations.evidence: Lists movement/running artifact, theta-associated high-gamma, type-2 movement ripples; says 'a non-trivial fraction that are peri-movement and that many in the field would not classify as SWRs' and 'higher false-positive rate'.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does NOT predict that raising speed_threshold yields fewer ripples — that is the opposite of how the filter works
expectations.evidence: Direction is unambiguous and correct throughout.
